title: ranunculus sardous var. angulatus
intro: Ranunculus sardous var. angulatus, also known as Water Crowfoot, is a variety of water crowfoot, a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant contains ranunculin, a toxic compound, but it also has medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and inflammation. The health benefits of Ranunculus sardous var. angulatus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into tea, tincture, or infused oil to utilize its medicinal properties, but caution should be exercised due to its toxicity.
